description: Change broadcast log. Every merge to main creates an entry here so ALL skills stay aware of changes across the system.

events.title: Staging auth checkpoint recorded with remote baseline and local-only verification state
events.summary: Shared repo memory now captures the exact staging-auth checkpoint after the Gemini remediation push. The checkpoint records that origin/main remained at commit 2415475 while newer local work restored tracked config safety, validated the local staging overlay, passed the real staging app-launch smoke, and identified the remaining sign-in smoke failure as an onboarding-path mismatch rather than a credential problem.

events.title: Gemini follow-up automation hardened and staging-credentials plan documented
events.summary: The integrity-cycle workflow now preserves the real integrity-check exit code, strict manual runs no longer masquerade as regressions, documentation-debt trend readiness counts only scheduled cycle snapshots, the contemporaneous logger now requires explicit retroactive markers, and the audit/trust docs now reflect the real status of Tier 1.1, Tier 2.1, Tier 2.2, Tier 3.1, and Tier 3.2. The staging preflight also records the exact remaining local auth-secret blockers without exposing their values.

events.title: Runtime smoke auto-resolves simulators and staging app-launch now passes
events.summary: The runtime smoke gate no longer depends on a single stale simulator UUID. It resolves a valid iOS Simulator destination at runtime, falls back to a named latest-OS device when CoreSimulator discovery is unavailable, and the real staging app_launch smoke now passes against iPhone 17 Pro. In parallel, DEBUG review mode now injects a seeded demo dataset so Home, Stats, Nutrition, Training, and Settings can render populated screenshot-friendly state without editing screenshots by hand.

events.title: Staging build path verified after CoreSimulator reset
events.summary: After restarting the user-domain CoreSimulatorService, the Staging iOS build succeeded end-to-end with the new xcconfig layer and opaque AppIcon catalog. The runtime smoke gate now fails only on the expected missing local prerequisite Config/Local/Staging.xcconfig, which confirms the remaining Tier 2.1 blocker is staging credentials rather than build infrastructure.

events.title: App icon renderer added and verify-ios HOME override removed
events.summary: The App Store icon pipeline now renders an opaque 1024x1024 marketing master from the checked-in FitMe PDF source, regenerates the full AppIcon catalog from that master, and the canonical verify-ios flow no longer redirects HOME/CFFIXED_USER_HOME into .build/xcode-home because Xcode asset catalogs and SwiftUI preview linking need the real CoreSimulator device set.

events.title: Staging xcconfig layer added and app-icon build blocker cleared
events.summary: The iOS target now reads runtime service configuration through tracked xcconfig files with untracked local overlays, a real Staging build configuration exists across app and test targets, staging smoke mode resolves to that configuration, and the missing AppIcon.appiconset PNG set was regenerated from the checked-in FitMe PDF source so builds no longer stop on the asset catalog.

events.title: Runtime smoke gates, contemporaneous logs, and documentation-debt baseline added
events.summary: Tier-2/Tier-3 Gemini audit follow-up groundwork landed: local-first runtime smoke profiles and runner, append-only contemporaneous feature logs, a generated documentation-debt report, and a control-room dashboard surface for baseline corpus debt.

events.title: Framework Measurement v6.0
events.description: Deterministic measurement instrumentation: phase timing (start/end/pause), cache hit tracking (L1/L2/L3), eval coverage gates, monitoring auto-sync, token counting, CU v2 continuous factors, rolling baselines, serial/parallel decomposition.

events.title: Control center alignment and IA refresh started
events.summary: The dashboard is being reorganized into a multi-workspace operating surface with shared control-center builders, a dedicated Knowledge tab, routed research/Figma workspaces, a standalone case-studies page, and cleaned current-truth roadmap references.

events.title: Framework docs deep-validation and next research chapters initialized
events.summary: The PM-flow and skills documentation were revalidated around the current v4.3 framework, a reusable auth runtime verification playbook was added, and new Phase 0 research chapters were opened for push notifications, app-store assets, import training plans, and AI engine architecture adaptation.

events.title: Marketing and Vercel truth cleanup chapter started
events.summary: README and the marketing website PRD were corrected to reflect that the website exists in code but is not truthfully launched as the canonical live surface, because the fit-tracker2 Vercel project is still rooted at dashboard and the site retains placeholder launch metadata.

events.title: Canonical iOS simulator build succeeded with GoogleSignIn resolved
events.summary: An elevated compile-only Debug build for the FitTracker iOS simulator target completed successfully after resolving the GoogleSignIn package, which confirms the repo-side auth wiring compiles even though real Google credentials and end-to-end auth validation are still pending.

events.title: Google auth moved from dormant source to repo-wired configuration gate
events.summary: The Xcode project now includes the GoogleSignIn package reference and GoogleAuthProvider target membership, Info.plist now includes placeholder Google client ID keys and URL scheme scaffolding, and runtime gating keeps Google hidden until valid local configuration is present.

events.title: Auth hardening switched email to the real provider gate and removed mock Google from the live UI
events.summary: The auth chapter now activates the real Supabase email provider whenever runtime credentials are present, exposes password reset in the auth UI, and stops presenting Google sign-in as a live option until the SDK and target wiring are actually complete.

events.title: Knowledge hub added to the control room and connected to canonical docs
events.summary: The control room now includes a dedicated reading surface for repo docs, shared PM framework files, and synced Notion/Linear/Vercel references, so the operator dashboard can open the actual source material instead of relying on summaries alone.
artifacts: Notion: Operations Control Room — Product Spec

events.title: Auth hardening chapter started as the next cleanup block
events.summary: FIT-6 is now in progress with a concrete execution brief, and a dedicated Notion chapter page now defines the scope for replacing mock or unavailable auth paths with production-backed Supabase auth.

events.title: Control room consolidated into fit-tracker2 and Speed Insights deployed
events.summary: The operations control room is now live on the canonical fit-tracker2 Vercel project, with both Web Analytics and Speed Insights deployed. The accidental standalone dashboard project remains as cleanup debt but is no longer the canonical live surface.

events.title: Operations control room deployed live on a separate dashboard Vercel project
events.summary: The updated control room was deployed successfully, but the deploy created or targeted a separate Vercel project named dashboard rather than updating the older fit-tracker2 project. The monitoring layer now records that split explicitly.

analytics: Vercel Web Analytics deployed with dashboard project

events.title: Live GitHub hydration and Vercel runtime visibility expanded in the control room
events.summary: The dashboard now hydrates GitHub issue truth when GITHUB_TOKEN is available, surfaces verified Vercel deployment/runtime state, and enables Vercel Web Analytics in the dashboard repo configuration without pretending that full production observability is already complete.

github: build-time issue hydration when GITHUB_TOKEN is present
analytics: Vercel Web Analytics adapter enabled in dashboard repo

events.title: GitHub repo truth and analytics health added to the external sync layer
events.summary: The shared external sync snapshot now includes canonical GitHub repo state and analytics instrumentation health, giving the control room a broader cross-system truth surface without faking live production telemetry.

events.title: Live Notion + Linear sync snapshot added to shared layer and control room
events.summary: The framework now records current Linear roadmap status and Notion status-page drift in a canonical shared file so the dashboard can show external-tool truth alongside repo truth.

notion: Case Study — Maintenance Cleanup + Operations Control Room

events.title: PM framework promoted to v4.3
events.summary: The self-healing hub now formally includes the operations control room, case-study monitoring, and maintenance-program orchestration as part of the framework itself.

events.title: Maintenance cleanup + operations control room initiated
events.summary: Cross-system cleanup established as a tracked program. Added dashboard control-room UI, case-study monitoring, and synchronized obvious repo-truth updates to Notion and Linear.

notion: Case Study — Maintenance Cleanup + Operations Control Room
figma: workflow surface planned, live dashboard integration pending

events.title: Google Gemini 2.5 Pro independent audit published verbatim on /trust
events.description: Different vendor, different model family, artifact-only access. Reviewed 65 case studies + 3 internal meta-analyses. Same-day correction appended for false-positive PR-vs-issue mix-up.

events.title: PM framework promoted to v7.1 (72h Integrity Cycle)
events.description: Recurring state.json audit via GitHub Actions cron. Baseline 40 features / 44 case studies / 0 findings.

events.title: PM framework promoted to v7.5 (Data Integrity Framework)
events.description: 8 cooperating defenses: write-time pre-commit (SCHEMA_DRIFT, PR_NUMBER_UNRESOLVED), 72h cycle (12 check codes), runtime smoke gates, contemporaneous logging, data-quality tiers, documentation-debt + measurement-adoption ledgers. 7 of 9 Gemini Tier items shipped.

events.title: PM framework promoted to v7.6 (Mechanical Enforcement)
events.description: 4 new write-time pre-commit check codes (PHASE_TRANSITION_NO_LOG, PHASE_TRANSITION_NO_TIMING, BROKEN_PR_CITATION write-time, CASE_STUDY_MISSING_TIER_TAGS) + per-PR review bot (pm-framework/pr-integrity status check) + weekly framework-status cron + 5 documented Class B gaps. 7 Class B → Class A promotions.

events.title: Developer Guide v1.0 → v7.6 published (745 lines)
events.description: Technical onboarding doc at docs/architecture/dev-guide-v1-to-v7-6.md. 16 sections covering 4 enforcement layers, state.json schema, phase lifecycle, dispatch, cache, measurement protocol, 12 integrity check codes, 3 operational walkthroughs. Mirrored at https://fitme-story.vercel.app/framework/dev-guide.

events.title: Tier 3.3 public external-replication invitation filed as GitHub issue #142
events.description: Pinned. The explicit final v7.6 deliverable per Phase 3c sequencing. Closes when at least one external case study lands at docs/case-studies/external/<handle>-<date>.md.

events.title: PR #141 merged (Codex v7.6 pending fixes)
events.description: 5 fix groups: state reconciliation + shared tracking + PR-bot tightening + doc alignment + test naming. Self-test on the PR exercised the new tightened semantics for the first time — passed (0/0 findings, all required exits 0).

events.title: Linear backfill (FIT-22 + FIT-6 → Done; FIT-44/45/46/47/48 created)
events.description: Linear MCP brought online. 5 new issues for v7.5, v7.6, PR #141, DEV guide, Tier 3.3 #142. 2 stale 'In Progress' issues marked Done after verifying state.json complete.

events.title: Notion backfill (new Framework v7.5+v7.6 sub-page + Project Context & Status v5.1→v7.6)
events.description: Notion MCP brought online. New sub-page under FitMe Product Hub captures full v7.5/v7.6 audit-response narrative. Project Context & Status updated from v5.1 framework + 44 features to v7.6 framework + 60 features (rows 50-60 added).

events.title: 19 PRDs remediated for CLAUDE.md rule #2 + readiness-score-v2.md created
events.description: Closes the 14 PRDs that previously failed rule #2 (no kill criteria). Adds concrete kill criteria + baselines with (T2 — Declared, 2026-04-26) tags. Adds v2 sections to 4 parent 18.x PRDs. Creates readiness-score-v2.md as standalone feature PRD. Documents 3 orphan PRDs with status banners. Commit 1492ec0.

validation_entry_schema.receiving_skill: skill that triggered the data flow

affected_skills: list of skills that read this field
